title: Signal
description: One observed fact about a founder or company. Nothing discarded: timestamped, source-tagged, deduplicated, trust-tiered at ingestion. Contract between the Memory track and the Intelligence track — change only by agreement.

source.description: Where the signal came from. 'application' and 'deck' are subject-supplied; treat accordingly.

observed_at.description: ISO 8601 — when the underlying event happened (commit date, post date, filing date).

ingested_at.description: ISO 8601 — when the system captured it. observed_at vs ingested_at gives trend-over-time, not just latest snapshot.

summary.description: What the signal says, one to three sentences. Verbatim quotes preferred over paraphrase.

raw_ref.description: Path or key to the archived raw artifact (fetched page, deck page, API response). Archiving the raw source is what promotes a signal up the trust ladder.

trust_tier.description: T1 verified-artifact: primary artifact on file and independently checked (repo code read, payment-processor export, signed contract). T2 verified-online: public source fetched and archived. T3 reconstructed: reference-class inference, labeled. T4 claimed: subject-supplied and unverified — the weakest tier. Verifying a claim (fetch + archive) promotes it.

authority.description: Orthogonal to trust_tier. subject: the founder/company's own material — never substantiates its own case. independent: third party. operator-primary: the investor's own notes/calls. agent-derived: output of this pipeline — material to argue with, never an authority.

dedupe_key.description: Stable key for dedup (url, content hash). Duplicate signals merge; their timestamps both survive in history.

contradicts.description: Signal ids this one contradicts. Contradictions are first-class — they become debate seeds and memo flags, not errors to suppress.
